# kinder — Full Documentation

> kind, but with everything you actually need.

kinder is a batteries-included tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It wraps kind (Kubernetes in Docker) and pre-installs production-ready addons: MetalLB, Envoy Gateway, Metrics Server, CoreDNS tuning, and the Headlamp dashboard.

Author: Patryk Golabek (https://patrykgolabek.dev)
Source: https://github.com/PatrykQuantumNomad/kinder
Documentation: https://kinder.patrykgolabek.dev

---

## Installation

Prerequisites:
- Go 1.25.7 or later
- Docker, Podman, or nerdctl
- kubectl

Build from source:

```sh
git clone https://github.com/PatrykQuantumNomad/kinder.git
cd kinder
make install
```

Verify installation:

```sh
kinder version
```

---

## Quick Start

### Step 1: Create a cluster

```sh
kinder create cluster
```

kinder provisions a local Kubernetes node and automatically installs all default addons (MetalLB, Envoy Gateway, Metrics Server, CoreDNS tuning, Headlamp dashboard).

### Step 2: Verify the node is ready

```sh
kubectl get nodes
```

### Step 3: Verify MetalLB is running

```sh
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
```

### Step 4: Verify Envoy Gateway is running

```sh
kubectl get pods -n envoy-gateway-system
```

### Step 5: Open the Headlamp dashboard

```sh
kubectl port-forward -n kube-system service/headlamp 8080:80
```

Open http://localhost:8080 in your browser. Retrieve the dashboard token:

```sh
kubectl get secret kinder-dashboard-token -n kube-system \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.token}' | base64 -d
```

### What You Get

| Addon | Namespace | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| MetalLB | metallb-system | LoadBalancer IP assignment |
| Envoy Gateway | envoy-gateway-system | Gateway API ingress |
| Metrics Server | kube-system | kubectl top support |
| CoreDNS tuning | kube-system | Optimised DNS caching |
| Headlamp | kube-system | Web UI for cluster inspection |

Delete the cluster:

```sh
kinder delete cluster
```

---

## Configuration Reference

kinder uses the same kind.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4 API version as kind. Pass a config file:

```sh
kinder create cluster --config cluster.yaml
```

### Addon Fields

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| metalLB | bool | true | Install MetalLB for LoadBalancer IP assignment |
| envoyGateway | bool | true | Install Envoy Gateway for Gateway API ingress |
| metricsServer | bool | true | Install Metrics Server for kubectl top support |
| coreDNSTuning | bool | true | Apply CoreDNS tuning for optimised local DNS caching |
| dashboard | bool | true | Install Headlamp web dashboard |

### Disabling Addons

```yaml
apiVersion: kind.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
addons:
  dashboard: false
  envoyGateway: false
```

### Skip All Addons

```yaml
apiVersion: kind.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
addons:
  metalLB: false
  envoyGateway: false
  metricsServer: false
  coreDNSTuning: false
  dashboard: false
```

Existing kind configuration files work without modification. The addons section is only processed by kinder.

---

## Addons

### MetalLB
LoadBalancer services with real external IPs from your Docker or Podman subnet. Automatically configured with an IP address pool from the container network range.

### Envoy Gateway
Gateway API routing with the eg GatewayClass pre-configured. Provides a standards-based ingress controller using the Kubernetes Gateway API.

### Metrics Server
Enables kubectl top and Horizontal Pod Autoscaler support. Installed with --kubelet-insecure-tls for local cluster compatibility.

### CoreDNS Tuning
Autopath short names, verified pod records, and doubled cache TTL. Improves DNS resolution performance for local development.

### Headlamp Dashboard
Web-based cluster UI accessible via port-forward. Token printed at cluster creation for authentication.
